channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How a wrong Google search can compromise your data and bring law enforcement calling
item.description: Wrong Google searches can not only compromise your device and personal data. They can also bring law enforcement to your doorstep. Hackers are targeting Google searches.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Chinese humanoid robot shows off its strength by lifting 35 pounds per hand
item.description: China's Shanghai Kepler Robotics has developed a new humanoid robot that can carry up to 35 pounds per hand for commercial applications across various industries
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Onion CEO explains Infowars purchase amid Alex Jones's claims about auction
item.description: The Onion CEO Ben Collins confirmed that the publication purchased Alex Jones’s Infowars outlet in a Saturday series of posts on the social media platform Bluesky amid Jones's claims of wrongdoing and illegal activity.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Amazon developing driver eyeglasses to shave seconds off deliveries
item.description: Such directions could shave valuable seconds off each delivery by providing left or right directions off elevators and around obstacles such as gates or aggressive dogs.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Australia is planning to block TikTok and Instagram for kids under 16
item.description: How do you remove children from the harms of social media? Politically the answer appears simple in Australia, but practically the solution could be far more difficult.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.5 gains security, networking upgrades
item.description: Red Hat is out this week with the latest milestone update of its flagship Linux platform. Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.5 (RHEL) became generally available on Nov.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: PCs with NPUs tweaked for AI now account for one of every five PCs shipped, says Canalys
item.description: One out of every five PCs shipped in the third quarter of 2024, a total of 13.3 million units, was a PC with a neural processing unit (NPU) fine-tuned for generative AI (genAI) development, according to data published Wednesday by analyst firm Canalys.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Millions in high speed internet grants awarded in rural New York
item.description: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer and Gov. Kathy Hochul announced the funds for open-access fiber, wireless hubs.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: JetBrains IDEs ease debugging for Kubernetes apps
item.description: JetBrains IDEs, with new 2024.3 edition releases, now provide a logical code structure view alongside the familiar physical code structure view in the Structure tool window.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: FBI probe into Chinese hacking finds 'broad and significant' espionage campaign
item.description: The U.S. government's ongoing probe into China-backed actors' attempts to hack into U.S. telecommunications infrastructure revealed a "broad and significant" espionage campaign targeted at those in government or political work, the FBI announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Solidigm unveils 122TB data center SSD for AI workloads
item.description: Solidigm, a provider of NAND flash solid-state drives, and memory giant Micron have each introduced very high-capacity SSD drives meant for enterprise data centers, particularly in AI use cases. Traditional hard disk drives have maintained their viability because of capacity advantages over SSDs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AMD cutting almost 1,000 jobs to focus resources on AI push
item.description: The move follows other cutbacks among longtime Silicon Valley companies. Cisco Systems Inc. is eliminating more than 6,000 positions, and Intel is cutting about 15% of its workforce of more than 100,000.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Only 8 monkeys remain free after more than a week outside a South Carolina compound
item.description: Authorities say Just eight monkeys remain free from the group who broke out of a South Carolina compound that breeds the primates for medical research.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In Cyprus, Ukrainians learn how to dispose of landmines that kill and maim hundreds
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — In a Cypriot National Guard camp, Ukrainians are being trained on how to identify, locate and dispose of landmines and other unexploded munitions that litter huge swaths of their country, killing and maiming hundreds of people, including children.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EU slaps Meta with a nearly 800 million euro fine for engaging in 'abusive' Marketplace practices
item.description: European Union regulators hit Facebook parent Meta with a fine of nearly 800 million euros on Thursday for what it calls “abusive practices” involving its Marketplace online classified ads business.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: One Tech Tip: Replacing passwords with passkeys for an easier login experience
item.description: LONDON (AP) — If you're tired of memorizing passwords, then give passkeys a try. You might have noticed that many online services are now offering the option of using passkeys, a digital authentication method touted as an easier and more secure way to log in.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: FBI, CISA say Chinese hackers breached multiple US telecom providers in targeted attack
item.description: The U.S. government said it is investigating after People's Republic of China hackers targeted commercial telecommunications service providers in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk says he and Trump have 'mandate to delete' regulations. Ethics laws could limit Musk role
item.description: In picking billionaire Elon Musk to be “our cost cutter” for the U.S. government, President-elect Donald Trump won't be the first American president to empower a business tycoon to look for ways to dramatically cut federal regulations. President Ronald Reagan tapped J.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Visual Studio 17.12 brings C++, Copilot enhancements
item.description: Visual Studio 2022 17.12, the latest version of Microsoft’s signature integrated development environment (IDE), is now generally available, with improvements ranging from productivity enhancements for C++ developers to integrated AI variable inspection in the GitHub Copilot AI-powered programming as
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Kotlin for Java developers
item.description: After Java, Kotlin is the most popular JVM language. Kotlin is an expressive, concise language with strong functional programming support. It’s especially appealing to Java developers because it is fully interoperable with Java, and the syntax is an easy transition.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Japan's SoftBank returns to profit after gains at Vision Fund and other investments
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology group SoftBank swung back to profitability in the July-September quarter, boosted by positive results in its Vision Fund investments. Tokyo-based SoftBank Group Corp.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why your AI models stumble before the finish line
item.description: In 2023, enterprises across industries invested heavily in generative AI proof of concepts (POCs), eager to explore the technology’s potential. Fast-forward to 2024, companies face a new challenge: moving AI initiatives from prototype to production.
item.dc:creator: Info World
